behavior_summary: U8P_MONEGG::hatch is an item process that checks an egg item's per-item map/flags field and, if not already processed, possibly spawns the egg's monster and marks the egg as processed.
The routine treats bit 0x80 in the item's map-array as a 'processed/hatched' flag: if 0x80 is set the process exits; on successful U8P_MONEGG::hatch the bit is set and written back.
U8P_MONEGG::hatch compares a monster identifier from MonsterEgg::getMonId(...) to a 5-bit value derived from Item::getMapArray(this) & 0x1F; if equal, MonsterEgg::hatch(...) is invoked.
After calling MonsterEgg::hatch, U8P_MONEGG::hatch sets the 0x80 processed bit and may destroy the item depending on Item::getFrame(this) (nonzero frame inferred to trigger Item::destroy).
U8P_MONEGG::leaveFastArea is a behavior invoked on fast-area exit that clears the 0x80 processed bit (writes back mapArray & 0x7F) so the egg can be re-evaluated later.
notable_control_flow: Guard: read Item::getMapArray(this) and return immediately if bit 0x80 is set (prevents repeated hatching).
Conditional U8P_MONEGG::hatch: only call MonsterEgg::hatch if MonsterEgg::getMonId(...) equals (Item::getMapArray(this) & 0x1F).
Post-U8P_MONEGG::hatch update: set 0x80 in the map-array and write it back via Item::setMapArray(this).
Cleanup branch: after marking processed, call Item::getFrame(this) and possibly Item::destroy(this) when frame indicates removal.
U8P_MONEGG::leaveFastArea clears the 0x80 bit unconditionally (mapArray & 0x7F -> Item::setMapArray) to reset the processed state on area exit.
open_questions: What gameplay meaning does the low 5-bit mask (0x1F) encode — current map id, egg variant, or a spawn-condition index? (inferred from context, confidence: low)
What specific values of Item::getFrame(this) cause Item::destroy(this)? The U8P_MONEGG::hatch code tests getFrame != 0 but exact semantics are not present in the snippet. (confidence: medium)
What is the intended lifetime semantics for the 0x80 bit across saves/loads and other engine events — persistent 'one-time hatched' marker or temporary per-area marker? (confidence: low)
The decompilation shows an expression '0x70h * 0x20h' in remnants — what higher-level logic or constant does this represent in the original source? (confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_MONEGG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Evaluate whether this egg item should U8P_MONEGG::hatch now; if not already marked, check a monster-id match condition, perform the U8P_MONEGG::hatch (spawn), mark the egg as processed (set 0x80), and possibly destroy the item.
entry_conditions: Runs as an item process (process type 0x0210) — invoked periodically or by engine evaluation for the egg item.
Only proceeds when the item's per-item map/flags does not have bit 0x80 set (i.e., not yet processed).

function_map.name: U8P_MONEGG::hatch
function_map.role: Item/process handler implementing egg U8P_MONEGG::hatch logic: guard by 0x80 flag, compare monster id to masked map-array, call MonsterEgg::hatch, mark processed, optional destroy

function_map.name: U8P_MONEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Behavior invoked on fast-area exit that clears the item's processed/hatched bit (bit 0x80) so the egg can be considered again later

external_calls.purpose: Read per-item map/flags to test 0x80 processed bit and to obtain low 5-bit value for id comparison
Obtain the monster identifier associated with this egg for equality check against the item's masked map-array value
Perform the actual U8P_MONEGG::hatch/spawn action when id/mask match and item is unprocessed
Write back the modified map/flags with the 0x80 processed bit set after a successful U8P_MONEGG::hatch
Inspect item frame/state to decide whether to destroy the item after hatching
Remove/destroy the egg item when Item::getFrame indicates it should be removed post-U8P_MONEGG::hatch
Read current per-item map/flags so it can be updated to clear the processed bit
Write back map-array masked with 0x7F to clear bit 0x80 on fast-area exit

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_MONEGG::hatch is an item process that checks an egg item's per-item map/flags field and, if not already processed, possibly spawns the egg's monster and marks the egg as processed.
